questions.q: The Adalaj Stepwell is located in which Indian state?
questions.explanation: The Adalaj Stepwell stands in the village of Adalaj in the state of Gujarat in western India.

questions.q: Adalaj is a type of which kind of structure?
questions.explanation: Adalaj is a stepwell, a water structure with descending steps leading down to the water table, known locally as a 'vav'.

questions.q: In which year was the Adalaj Stepwell built?
questions.explanation: The Adalaj Stepwell was completed in 1498, as recorded in inscriptions at the site.

questions.q: Adalaj Stepwell is located close to which major Gujarati city?
questions.explanation: The stepwell lies in Adalaj village a short distance north of Ahmedabad, near Gandhinagar.

questions.q: How many storeys deep does the Adalaj Stepwell descend?
questions.explanation: The Adalaj Stepwell descends five storeys below ground level toward the water.

questions.q: What is the local Gujarati word for a stepwell like Adalaj?
questions.explanation: Stepwells are called 'vav' in Gujarati (and 'baoli' or 'baori' in other parts of India).

questions.q: What was the primary practical purpose of the Adalaj Stepwell?
options: A water source and cool resting place
questions.explanation: Stepwells like Adalaj provided water and a cool, shaded retreat for travellers during the hot, dry months.

questions.q: The Adalaj Stepwell is celebrated for blending the artistic motifs of which two traditions?
questions.explanation: Adalaj is famous for combining Hindu and Islamic decorative motifs in a single Indo-Islamic design.

questions.q: What is the dominant building material of the Adalaj Stepwell?
questions.explanation: The stepwell is built and intricately carved from sandstone.

questions.q: Compared with the surface above, the lower galleries of the stepwell are notably what?
questions.explanation: The deep underground galleries stay several degrees cooler than the surface, offering relief from the heat.

questions.q: The Adalaj Stepwell is most closely associated with which queen who had it built?
questions.explanation: The stepwell was commissioned in memory and honour of Rana Veer Singh by his widow, Queen Rudabai (Rudadevi).

questions.q: Queen Rudabai was the widow of a ruler from which dynasty?
questions.explanation: Rudabai was the queen of Rana Veer Singh of the Vaghela dynasty, who ruled the small kingdom of Dandai Desh.

questions.q: What is the geometric plan of the central well shaft and many platforms at Adalaj?
questions.explanation: Adalaj is distinctive for its octagonal plan at the landings, with carved pillars supporting the platforms.

questions.q: How many entrance stairways lead down into the Adalaj Stepwell?
questions.explanation: Three stepped entrances from different directions descend and meet at the first underground storey, an unusual feature.

questions.q: The construction of the Adalaj Stepwell was begun under whose initiative before Rudabai completed it?
questions.explanation: Rana Veer Singh had started the project, and after his death in battle it was finished, with the stepwell named for his widow Queen Rudabai.

questions.q: According to tradition, the region of Adalaj at the time was being contested with a Muslim ruler of Gujarat known as what?
questions.explanation: Legend links the stepwell's completion to the period of Mahmud Begada, the Sultan of Gujarat who controlled the area.

questions.q: Which carved motif depicting a 'tree of life' is found in the ornamentation of Adalaj?
questions.explanation: The carvings include the Kalpavriksha, the wish-fulfilling tree of life, among Adalaj's celebrated decorative themes.

questions.q: A famous carving at Adalaj shows the 'Ami Khumbor', which represents what?
options: A pot containing the water of life
questions.explanation: The Ami Khumbor is a carved pot symbolising the water of life, reflecting the well's life-giving role.

questions.q: The carved galleries of Adalaj depict scenes of which kind of subject matter?
options: Everyday life, dancers, musicians and animals
questions.explanation: The friezes show everyday scenes such as women churning buttermilk, dancers, musicians and animals, alongside floral patterns.

questions.q: Why does the carved decoration of Adalaj generally avoid large human or deity figures on the deepest, most sacred levels in the Islamic-influenced areas?
options: Islamic tradition discouraged figural depiction, favouring geometric and floral patterns
The stone was too soft to carve figures
The carvers ran out of time
It was forbidden by the British
questions.explanation: Islamic artistic tradition favoured geometric, floral and calligraphic ornament over figural imagery, which influenced parts of Adalaj's decoration.

questions.q: Stepwells such as Adalaj were built mainly to cope with which climatic challenge of the region?
options: Long dry seasons and scarce water
questions.explanation: In Gujarat's arid climate with long dry seasons, stepwells stored water and made it accessible year-round.

questions.q: Roughly how deep is the Adalaj Stepwell from the surface down to the water level?
questions.explanation: Descending five storeys, the stepwell reaches a depth of roughly 20 metres to the water.

questions.q: The kingdom ruled by Rana Veer Singh, for whom Adalaj was begun, is traditionally named in the legend as what?
questions.explanation: Rana Veer Singh is described in the legend as the Vaghela ruler of the small kingdom of Dandai Desh.

questions.q: In the popular legend, what tragic fate is said to have befallen Queen Rudabai after the stepwell was completed?
options: She jumped into the well to her death rather than marry the conquering sultan
questions.explanation: Legend holds that Rudabai, having been promised in marriage to the sultan in exchange for finishing the well, threw herself into it to escape the union.

questions.q: The architectural style of Adalaj, drawing on the Solanki-era temple tradition of the region, is broadly known by which name?
questions.explanation: The carving tradition follows the Maru-Gurjara (Solanki) style of western India, fused here with Islamic ornamental elements.

questions.q: Inscriptions at Adalaj recording its construction are written primarily in which language?
questions.explanation: A Sanskrit inscription at the site records the year 1498 and the patronage associated with the stepwell.

questions.q: An unusual social and architectural feature of Adalaj is that its three stairways converge at which point?
options: At the very bottom near the water
At the first underground storey forming a single platform
questions.explanation: The three entrance stairs meet at a large platform on the first storey underground, a layout rare among Indian stepwells.

questions.q: Which body is responsible for protecting and maintaining the Adalaj Stepwell as a monument today?
options: The Archaeological Survey of India / state heritage authorities
questions.explanation: Adalaj is a protected heritage monument cared for under the Archaeological Survey of India and state heritage authorities.

questions.q: Beyond supplying water, stepwells such as Adalaj also served what important social and spiritual function?
options: A gathering and resting place with religious significance for travellers and women
questions.explanation: Stepwells were communal gathering places of social and religious importance, especially for women who came to fetch water and offer prayers.

questions.q: Another famous Gujarat stepwell, often compared with Adalaj and inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site, is which one?
questions.explanation: Rani ki Vav at Patan in Gujarat, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, is the most celebrated stepwell often compared with Adalaj.
